definitions: 推翻；颠覆；打倒（尤指推翻政府、政权或制度）

examples.text: The company launched propaganda campaigns against Arbenz and called on its deep connections in the US Government for help. Citing fears of communism, the CIA orchestrated the overthrow of the democratically elected Arbenz in 1954. That same year in Honduras, thousands of United Fruit workers went on strike.
examples.explanation: 这里的 overthrow 是名词，表示“推翻、颠覆（政权或领导人）”。句中 orchestrated the overthrow of ... 表示“策划并实施了对……的推翻”。整句意思是：以对共产主义的担忧为由，中情局在 1954 年策划推翻了民选的阿本斯。

source.text: The dark history of bananas - John Soluri
